Name: Retatrutide
Short description: A triple-agonist peptide targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors for advanced metabolic research. For research use only.
Description:
Retatrutide is a novel investigational triple-agonist peptide that simultaneously targets GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors. This tri-receptor approach represents the latest generation of metabolic research compounds, building on the dual-agonist framework established by earlier peptides.
\n
\nEach vial contains lyophilized retatrutide powder at the selected strength. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.
Meta: _ap_disclaimer: For research use only. Not for human or veterinary use. Not a drug, food, or cosmetic. Not for diagnostic or therapeutic use.

Name: Tirzepatide
Short description: A dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist peptide for metabolic and endocrine research. For research use only.

Name: Tesamorelin
Short description: A synthetic GHRH analog peptide studied for its role in growth hormone release and body composition research. For research use only.
Description:

Name: MOTS-c
Short description: A mitochondrial-derived peptide studied for its role in cellular energy metabolism and exercise mimetic research. For research use only.
Description:
MOTS-c (Mitochondrial Open Reading Frame of the Twelve S rRNA type-c) is a 16-amino-acid peptide encoded by mitochondrial DNA. It is one of a small number of known mitochondrial-derived peptides (MDPs) and has attracted significant research interest for its role in regulating metabolic homeostasis and AMPK pathway activation.
\n

Name: BPC-157
Short description: A pentadecapeptide derived from gastric juice proteins, studied extensively in tissue repair and cytoprotection research. For research use only.
Description:
BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound-157) is a 15-amino-acid peptide derived from a portion of the human gastric juice protein. It is one of the most widely studied peptides in the tissue repair and cytoprotection research space, with a broad range of published studies examining its role in wound healing, tendon repair, and gastrointestinal protection.

Name: NAD+
Short description: Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide — a coenzyme central to cellular energy metabolism and sirtuin activation research. For research use only.
Description:
NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide) is a coenzyme present in all living cells that plays a central role in energy metabolism, DNA repair, and sirtuin activation. Research into NAD+ supplementation has expanded rapidly, with studies examining its potential in age-related decline, mitochondrial function, and neuroprotection.
\n
\nEach vial contains lyophilized NAD+ powder. Reconstitute with bacteriostatic water before use in research applications.

Name: SS-31
Short description: A mitochondrial-targeted tetrapeptide studied for its role in oxidative stress reduction and mitochondrial membrane stabilization. For research use only.
Description:
SS-31 (also known as elamipretide or Bendavia) is a cell-permeable tetrapeptide that selectively targets the inner mitochondrial membrane. It binds to cardiolipin, a phospholipid critical to electron transport chain function, and has been studied for its role in reducing reactive oxygen species production and protecting mitochondrial structure.
